title: ranunculus orientalis subsp. hierosolymitanus
intro: Ranunculus orientalis subsp. hierosolymitanus, also known as Jerusalem buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and alkaloids. The health benefits of Ranunculus orientalis subsp. hierosolymitanus include its potential use in treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its flowers, leaves, or roots, and can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, or infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
